_comment: Adversarial fixture for the question-bank gate. Spans are AUTHORED test data (public-standard facts phrased here), not source reproductions. Each case is self-contained: a question + the candidate spans it could cite. The gate must reach `expect` for each.

cases.name: clean_compliance_pass
cases.why: lane matches, quote is verbatim, and the quote supports the answer

question.stem: What is the minimum hot-holding temperature for hot foods?

spans.text: Hot foods must be held at 135°F or higher to remain safe for service.

cases.name: semantic_inversion_fail
cases.why: verbatim + lane PASS, but the quote states a MINIMUM and the stem asks the MAXIMUM — only the semantic judge can catch this

question.stem: What is the maximum safe temperature at which hot food may be held?
options: There is no maximum hold temperature

spans.text: Hot foods must be held at 135°F or higher to remain safe for service.

cases.name: cross_lane_fail
cases.why: THE KEYSTONE: a PRODUCT (POS) question citing a COMPLIANCE span that merely shares the word 'item'. Must fail lane-match with zero model calls.

question.stem: In the POS, how does a cashier void an item after payment is complete?
options: Press Void on the Sales screen

spans.text: Discard any food item left in the temperature danger zone for more than four hours.

cases.name: fabricated_quote_fail
cases.why: lane matches but the quote is not present in the cited span — fabricated/hallucinated quote, caught deterministically

question.stem: Within how long must food be cooled from 135°F to 70°F?
question.quote: cool food from 135°F to 70°F within two hours

spans.text: Hot foods must be held at 135°F or higher to remain safe for service.

cases.name: clean_product_pass
cases.why: product-lane question, verbatim quote from a product span, quote supports the answer

question.stem: What is required to void a completed transaction at the POS?

spans.text: Cashiers can void a completed transaction from the Sales screen using the Void button; a manager PIN is required to confirm.
